title: AST DUI: Palmer [AK25052458]
summary: On 06/01/2025 at approximately 0901 hours, Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop on the Parks Hwy near Trunk Road in Palmer for moving violations. title: AST Motor Vehicle Collision - Injury: Palmer [AK25052451]
summary: On 06/01/2025 at approximately 0804 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to a solo motorcycle crash near mile 7 Old Glenn Hwy in Palmer. Minor injuries were reported. Alcohol impairment was a factor and investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST REDDI: Glennallen [AK25052422]
summary: On 06/01/2025 at approximately 0148 hours, Troopers received a report of an intoxicated female attempting to drive toward Anchorage. Troopers were unable to immediately locate the female.

title: AST Assault: Koyukuk [AK25052269]
summary: On 5/31/25 at approximately 1431 hours, the Alaska State Troopers were notified of an assault in the village of Koyukuk. Two TPOs witnessed Percy Lolntiz II assaulting his mother with a rolling pin. Lolnitz fought with TPOs during the apprehension. Lolnitz was arrested and transported to the Fairban...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Violating Conditions of Release: Fairbanks [AK25050617]
summary: On May 27, 2025, Larry Moseley was arrested for Violating his Conditions of Release after the incident reported by the victim was confirmed through investigation by AST. Larry was remanded to FCC that day.

title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent: May 29, 2025
summary: May 29, 2025: The Arctic sea ice has experienced a greater decrease for this week of 3.54% (last week’s decrease rate was 2.41%), from the level on 05/22/2025 of 12.246 M km 2 to 11.813 M km 2 for this week. Plot Compiled by: Howard J. Diamond, PhD; Climate Science Program Manager at NOAA’s Air Reso...

title: BBQ Month 2025
summary: WHEREAS, Alaska summers offer longer days and abundant opportunities to enjoy the outdoors with family and friends; and WHEREAS, barbecuing is one such opportunity, being a distinctly American pastime that embodies the spirit of summer, bringing together loved ones and neighbors to share delicious f...

title: Tourism Season in Alaska: How Small Businesses Can Get Ready
summary: Summer in Alaska means one thing: visitors. Lots of them. According to the Alaska Travel Industry Association’s 2023-24 annual report , visitors to Alaska generated $5.6 billion in total economic output, $3.9 billion in direct visitor spending to Alaska businesses, and supported over 48,000 jobs. That’s a massive opportunity for small businesses...
